title: SAIL Safety Philosophy
point: Nothing is more important than Safety and Health in SAIL.

title: Business Excellence
point: Excellence in Safety and Health yields excellent business results.

title: Continuous Improvement
point: SAIL continuously improves safety and health management systems.

title: Ultimate Goal
point: Ultimate aim is a safe and healthy workplace.

title: Safety Coverage
point: Safety is adhered to in all plant and unit operations.

title: Apex Monitoring
point: Safety issues are monitored at Board level.

title: HSE Oversight
point: Board Sub-committee on Health Safety and Environment oversees safety.

title: Opening Agenda
point: Safety and Health issues are discussed first in management forums.

title: SAIL Vision
point: SAIL aims to become an industry leader in safety performance.

title: Corporate Safety Policy
point: SAIL has a Corporate Safety Policy and Guiding Principles.

title: Stakeholder Coverage
point: Safety policy covers employees contractors and stakeholders.

title: Community Coverage
point: Safety policy includes neighboring communities around plants and mines.

title: Policy Alignment
point: All safety activities are planned in line with Safety Policy.

title: OH&S Objectives
point: Safety and health objectives are planned in advance.

title: Programme Support
point: Objectives are achieved through OH&S management programmes.

title: Performance Growth
point: SAIL has achieved steady improvement in safety performance.

title: Occupational Health Growth
point: Occupational health indicators show consistent improvement.

title: ISO 45001
point: ISO 45001 relates to Occupational Health and Safety Management.

title: ISO 9001
point: ISO 9001 relates to Quality Management.

title: ISO 14001
point: ISO 14001 relates to Environmental Management.

title: PDCA Philosophy
point: SAIL follows Plan Do Check Act philosophy.

title: Continual Improvement
point: PDCA promotes continual improvement.

title: Leadership Role
point: Effective leadership is critical for positive safety culture.

title: Zero Harm
point: Positive safety culture supports zero harm objective.

title: Top-Level Monitoring
point: Safety performance is monitored by Board Chairman and Directors.

title: Quarterly Monitoring
point: BSC on HSE reviews safety quarterly.

title: Daily Monitoring
point: Directors and Executive Directors monitor safety daily.

title: SED
point: Safety Engineering Department ensures operational safety.

title: SED Presence
point: Every plant and unit has a Safety Engineering Department.

title: SED Function
point: SED ensures safe operations and maintenance activities.

title: Fire Services Role
point: Fire Services Department handles emergency response.

title: Emergency Readiness
point: Fire Services remain alert for rescue and relief operations.

title: Fire Prevention
point: Fire Services monitor fire prevention measures.

title: DSO
point: Departmental Safety Officer monitors shop-level safety.

title: Safety Steward Role
point: Safety Steward ensures compliance with safety standards.

title: Daily Monitoring by DSO
point: DSOs monitor safety aspects daily.

title: SED Coordination
point: DSOs work closely with Safety Engineering Department.

title: ZSO
point: Zonal Safety Officers monitor project safety.

title: Project Monitoring
point: ZSOs are deployed in project and expansion areas.

title: Three Es
point: Engineering Education and Enforcement are the 3 Es of accident prevention.

title: Accident Prevention Basis
point: SAIL safety management hinges on the 3 Es.

title: Engineering Controls
point: Safety through built-in design features.

title: Design Stage Safety
point: Safety features are incorporated during design stage.

title: Safety Interlocks
point: Interlocks prevent unsafe machine operation.

title: Limit Switches
point: Limit switches are engineering safety measures.

title: Alarms
point: Alarms warn against abnormal conditions.

title: Detectors
point: Detectors identify hazardous situations.

title: CCTV
point: CCTV supports safety monitoring.

title: Fire Retardant Paint
point: Fire retardant paint protects cables.

title: Engineering Measure Purpose
point: Engineering controls protect men and machines.

title: Periodic Checking
point: Safety devices are periodically checked.

title: Education Element
point: Education focuses on competency building.

title: Training Target
point: At least one-third workforce is covered in training.

title: Continuous Training
point: Safety training is conducted continuously.

title: CHRD Training
point: Training programmes are organized through CHRD.

title: Training Coverage
point: Training includes safety and health topics.

title: Statutory Training
point: Statutory requirements are part of training modules.

title: Gas Safety Training
point: Gas safety is a regular training topic.

title: Electrical Safety Training
point: Electrical safety is a regular training topic.

title: Crane Safety Training
point: Crane safety is covered in training.

title: Conveyor Safety Training
point: Conveyor safety is included in training.

title: Material Handling Training
point: Material handling safety is taught.

title: BBS
point: Behavior Based Safety focuses on safe behavior.

title: Occupational Hygiene Training
point: Occupational hygiene forms part of training.

title: First Aid Training
point: First aid is included in safety programmes.

title: Stress Management
point: Stress management training is provided.

title: Disease Prevention
point: Occupational disease prevention is covered.

title: LEO
point: LEO means Learning From Each Other.

title: LEO Purpose
point: LEO workshops encourage sharing and learning.

title: Industry Participation
point: LEO workshops include other steel producers.

title: Large Group Interaction
point: Large Group Interactions enhance learning.

title: Departmental Training Centres
point: Department-level training centres provide technical training.

title: Contract Worker Training
point: Contract workers receive induction training.

title: Induction Duration
point: Contract worker induction lasts two days.

title: Job-Specific Training
point: Workers receive job-specific hazard training.

title: Risk Awareness
point: Training covers hazards risks and controls.

title: Height Work Training
point: Practical training is provided for work at height.

title: Roof Sheeting Training
point: Special demonstrations are provided for roof work.

title: Training Rig
point: Special training rigs are used for demonstrations.

title: Feedback System
point: Training feedback is collected for improvement.

title: Enforcement Element
point: Enforcement ensures adherence to procedures.

title: SOP Integration
point: Safety requirements are integrated into SOPs.

title: SOP
point: Standard Operating Procedure.

title: SMP
point: Safe Maintenance Procedure.

title: WI
point: Work Instruction.

title: Technological Discipline
point: SOPs SMPs and WIs maintain discipline.

title: Periodic Review
point: Procedures are reviewed periodically.

title: Technology Changes
point: Documents are updated with technology changes.

title: Incident Learning
point: Documents are revised after incidents.

title: KM Portal Access
point: Updated documents are available on KM portals.

title: Permit to Work
point: System for controlling hazardous jobs.

title: PTW Usage
point: Permit to Work is mandatory for hazardous work.

title: Protocol System
point: Protocols provide safeguards for critical jobs.

title: Multiple Agency Work
point: Protocols are used when multiple agencies are involved.

title: Emergency Preparedness Plan
point: Plan for handling emergency situations.

title: Mock Drills
point: Mock drills assess preparedness.

title: Multi-Agency Drills
point: Drills involve several connected departments.

title: Gas Safety Participation
point: Gas Safety teams participate in drills.

title: OHSC Participation
point: OHSC participates in emergency preparedness drills.

title: PPE

point: PPE use is mandatory for employees and contractors.

title: Safety Shoes
point: Safety shoes are standard PPE.

title: Safety Helmet
point: Safety helmet is mandatory PPE.

title: Ear Plugs
point: Ear protection prevents hearing damage.

title: Nose Mask
point: Nose masks protect respiratory health.

title: Fire Retardant Garments
point: Used for fire protection.

title: Full Body Harness
point: Used during work at height.

title: Arc Flash Suit
point: Protects against electrical arc hazards.

title: Road Safety Enforcement
point: Road safety norms are strictly enforced.

title: Gas Safety Enforcement
point: Gas safety norms are strictly enforced.

title: Height Safety Enforcement
point: Height safety norms are strictly enforced.

title: Hot Metal Safety
point: Special precautions apply to hot metal and slag.

title: Electrical Safety Enforcement
point: Electrical safety is continuously monitored.

title: Material Handling Safety
point: Material handling norms are enforced.

title: HIRA
point: Hazard Identification and Risk Assessment.

title: HIRA Coverage
point: HIRA covers most operation and maintenance activities.

title: Risk Control Hierarchy
point: Risks are controlled using hierarchy of controls.

title: ALARP
point: As Low As Reasonably Practicable.

title: ALARP Goal
point: Risks must be reduced to acceptable limits.

title: Factories Act Compliance
point: Compliance with Factories Act is ensured.

title: Mines Act Compliance
point: Compliance with Mines Act is ensured.

title: Electricity Act Compliance
point: Compliance with Electricity Act is ensured.

title: Petroleum Act Compliance
point: Compliance with Petroleum Act is ensured.

title: Explosives Act Compliance
point: Compliance with Explosives Act is ensured.

title: Incident Investigation
point: All work-related incidents are investigated.

title: Root Cause Analysis
point: Investigation seeks underlying causes.

title: Corrective Action
point: Corrective actions prevent recurrence.

title: Preventive Action
point: Preventive actions reduce future risk.

title: Control Hierarchy
point: Controls are selected according to hierarchy.

title: Employee Participation
point: Joint participation supports safety culture.

title: Committee Coverage
point: All employees are covered by safety committees.

title: Hazard Consultation
point: Employees are consulted on hazards.

title: Hazard Mitigation
point: Workers participate in hazard mitigation.

title: Apex Safety Committee
point: Statutory bipartite safety forum.

title: Departmental Safety Committee
point: Departmental safety issues are reviewed.

title: Pit Safety Committee
point: Pit safety committee functions in mines.

title: Union Participation
point: Trade unions participate in committees.

title: Scheduled Meetings
point: Safety committee meetings are scheduled.

title: OH&S Improvement
point: Committee discussions drive OH&S improvement.

title: Three-Tier Audit
point: SAIL uses a three-tier safety audit system.

title: Internal Audit
point: Internal audits involve Area Safety Officer and DSO.

title: SSO Audit
point: Second tier audit is conducted by SSO.

title: Third Party Audit
point: External agencies conduct statutory audits.

title: Apex Inspection
point: HODs Sectional Incharges and DSOs conduct apex inspections.

title: Cross-Functional Inspection
point: Multiple departments participate in inspections.

title: Civil Department Role
point: Civil department participates in safety inspections.

title: Electrical Department Role
point: Electrical department participates in inspections.

title: Crane Engineering Role
point: Crane Engineering participates in inspections.

title: Structural Inspection Role
point: Structural inspection teams participate.

title: Fire Safety Inspection
point: Fire Services conduct fire safety inspections.

title: Behavioural Intervention
point: Identification of unsafe acts and conditions.

title: Safety Review
point: Periodic safety review occurs at top management level.

title: Review Participants
point: DICs Head of Works and HODs review safety.

title: Strategic Action Plans
point: Reviews generate action plans.

title: Continuous Improvement Focus
point: Reviews aim at continuous improvement.

title: SSO Formation
point: SAIL Safety Organization formed in 1988.

title: SSO Purpose
point: SSO coordinates safety and fire service activities.

title: Safety Culture Development
point: SSO promotes safety culture across plants.

title: Policy Development
point: SSO develops safety policies and procedures.

title: Audit Responsibility
point: SSO conducts periodic audits.

title: Training Responsibility
point: SSO builds employee competence.

title: Performance Monitoring
point: SSO organizes review meetings.

title: Fatal Incident Study
point: SSO studies fatal and serious incidents.

title: Data Management
point: SSO maintains accident databases.

title: JCSSI Secretariat
point: SSO performs secretarial functions for JCSSI.

title: External Liaison
point: SSO liaises with MoS NSC WSA and others.

title: Awareness Promotion
point: SSO spreads safety awareness through publications.

title: Safety Consultant Deployment
point: Safety consultants deployed at five integrated steel plants.

title: Behavior Focus
point: Consultants address behavioural safety issues.

title: Industry Representation

point: IPSS includes wagon tippling standards.
